heading: Corps of Engineers: detail of officers to assist Mayor of District of Columbia
label: 7214 Corps of Engineers: detail of officers to assist Mayor of District of Columbia
text: The President may detail not more than three officers assigned to the Corps of Engineers to assist the Mayor of the District of Columbia in discharging his duties.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)3534(a)3534(b)10:189 (less 1st sentence).10:190.(48th through 56th words of 1st sentence, and 2d sentence), .June 11, 1878, ch. 180, § 220 Stat. 103(last sentence); restated (proviso under “For Engineer’s Office”), .June 11, 1878, ch. 180, § 5Aug. 7, 1894, ch. 23228 Stat. 246(words after last semicolon of 1st par. under “General Expenses”), .Mar. 3, 1881, ch. 13421 Stat. 460, J. Res. 7 (last sentence), .Dec. 24, 189026 Stat. 1113In subsection (a), the words “whose grade is above first lieutenant” are substituted for the words “from among the captains or officers of higher grade”. The words “in the discretion of” and “from time to time, from the Corps of Engineers, by the President, for this duty” are omitted as surplusage. 10:189 (last sentence) is omitted as obsolete.In subsections (a) and (b), the words “assigned to” are substituted for the word “of”, since, under , officers are assigned to, rather than commissioned in, the Corps of Engineers.section 3063 of this titleIn subsection (b), the words “assist that officer” are substituted for the words “act as assistants to said engineer commissioner”. The words “his duties” are substituted for the words “the special duties imposed upon him”.
